rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless): auto-checkable assertions pass (loads-clean, calls-real-inference, declares-*, inference-off-main-thread, shared-auto-init-loader, live-status-region, errors-on-the-page, images-have-alt, buttons-have-names). Real full-lifecycle run in headless Chromium with a persistent profile: first visit showed state=download-required with a 'Download model (~80 MB)' button; after downloading, pipeline('text2text-generation','ldenoue/Title_Generation_T5Small_Model',{device:'wasm',dtype:'q8'}) produced REAL output for the default article — 'summarize: The web platform keeps gaining new capabilities…' → 'Web platform keeps gaining new capabilities'. 0 console errors. Sampling verified: 4 draws at temperature 1.1 gave 4 DIFFERENT candidate headlines ('Google Maps Exploring Data…', 'Data Protection: An extensive network…', 'The idea behind the internet based apps…', 'browser extension - Microsoft Windows Phone'); beam gave a distinct 'best' line.
rubric.notes: T5-small title fine-tune (lineage: fabiochiu/t5-*-medium-title-generation on the 190k Medium-articles dataset). The training convention prepends T5's 'summarize:' prefix; the worker applies it. This is TITLE generation (one catchy line), distinct from summarisation and from the instruction-tuned FLAN-T5 / prefix-driven T5-base demos.

rubric.evidence: conformance: io-shape-semantic=manual. Output is a short single-line headline appropriate to the article (verified samples above). Beam ('best') is deterministic and coherent; sampled candidates are shorter, varied, sometimes rough (t5-small at q8) — shown honestly as candidates, never presented as a single ground truth. Per-candidate token counts and the input-token count are displayed.
rubric.notes: Quality is that of a small (t5-small) title model at q8: the beam headline is reliably sensible; sampling deliberately trades coherence for variety, which is the point of the A/B use case.

rubric.evidence: conformance: declares-model-id (ldenoue/Title_Generation_T5Small_Model, linked to card), declares-task (text2text-generation), declares-backend (WASM), declares-quantisation (q8), inference-off-main-thread (Web Worker: headline.js HeadlineEngine → worker.js) all pass. At-a-glance also documents the input convention (summarize: prefix), architecture, ~80 MB download, and MIT license.
rubric.notes: Runnability was verified before building: no monoT5-style generative reranker ships ONNX, but this T5 title fine-tune ships a full transformers.js ONNX (onnx/encoder_model_quantized.onnx + onnx/decoder_model_merged_quantized.onnx) and loads/runs on WASM q8.

rubric.evidence: conformance: shared-auto-init-loader=pass; auto-init-on-cached=manual, VERIFIED in a single continuous session: first visit showed the Download control (download-required); after the download completed and cache warmed, a fresh tab to the same page auto-initialised to state=ready ('Model ready — running locally on your device.') with NO Download button (everSawDownloadButton=false) and produced real output, 0 console errors.
rubric.notes: Uses shared createModelLoader (lib/model-loader.js); no hand-rolled Load button. A per-model 'Clear cached model' control is offered once ready.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region, errors-on-the-page, runs-without-webgpu-fallback all pass + honest-progress-retry-offline=manual. Download progress renders in the shared loader progress bar; run failures surface as a .status.err message ('Generation failed: …'); the createModelLoader Retry path covers load recovery. The WASM model needs no WebGPU. A short-input guard shows a readable error instead of running.
rubric.notes: Per-run status uses role=status aria-live=polite; the streamed best headline and candidate list are aria-live regions.

rubric.evidence: conformance: controls-operable=manual. Verified controls (across model page + ladder): sample-article chips (role=group); Best/Candidates mode toggle (aria-pressed segmented buttons) that reveals the sampling controls; range sliders for candidate count, temperature, top-k, and max headline length, each with a live value label bound on input; the Write-headline / Generate-candidates / Run-the-pipeline buttons; the practical page's candidate list is a role=listbox of role=option rows, selectable by click or keyboard (Enter/Space) with copy-to-clipboard. Driving the run button produced the real headline above; all four ladder pages loaded with 0 console errors (multi-model resolves the cross-model SummarizeEngine import and mounts 2 loaders).
rubric.notes: Every field has an associated <label>; buttons have focus-visible outlines; sliders use accent-color.

rubric.evidence: conformance: no-fake-output=manual. The only headlines shown are streamed/decoded from the pipeline's generate(); empty output renders '(empty headline)'. The 'exact fed string' box + SentencePiece token chips are computed from the same PREFIX + article the worker feeds the encoder, so what you read is what runs. Bundled sample articles are written for this demo (no external/rights-encumbered source). No canned strings anywhere.

conformance: images-have-alt=pass (no ; the favicon is an inline SVG data URI), buttons-have-names=pass. Mode toggle uses aria-pressed; sample chips + generation-mode groups use role=group with aria-label; the decode-timeline canvas has an aria-label; status/results use role=status aria-live=polite; the candidate listbox uses role=listbox / role=option / aria-selected with keyboard operation.
rubric.notes: Applied accessibility guidance: aria-pressed segmented control, single polite live regions for async status, native <label> association, accessible names on all controls.

rubric.evidence: conformance: responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass; parity-both-classes=manual. responsive-check.mjs: desktop (1280×800) clean, mobile (360×740 DPR3) clean. Agent measured horizontal overflow directly at 360×740 DPR3: after a targeted fix (overflow-wrap:anywhere on the at-a-glance cells + loader detail, since the model id 'ldenoue/Title_Generation_T5Small_Model' is a long unbreakable token) overflow dropped from 87px to 3px on the model page and 0px on all four ladder pages; the residual 3px is a single inline reference link at the edge (the API code block scrolls inside its own overflow-x:auto <pre>, not the page). Agent read screenshots (scratchpad/headline-shots/): light (warm paper + indigo) and dark both legible on desktop & mobile; the long model id now wraps inside the table.
rubric.notes: Primary control buttons/sliders use min-block-size≈2.2rem; the controls/candidate/two-column layouts use flex-wrap + repeat(auto-fit, minmax(...)). Sub-24px flags from responsive-check are inline crumb/reference text links, not primary tap targets.

rubric.evidence: conformance: inp-stays-low=manual. Inference runs in a Web Worker (HeadlineEngine → worker.js); the best headline streams via a TextStreamer so the control UI stays responsive during generation, and candidate draws happen off-thread in a loop. The live 'fed string' preview is debounced (120ms) and O(input); token-chip render is bounded by the (short) input length.
rubric.notes: Applied identify-inp-causes / break-up-long-tasks: off-main-thread inference + streamed incremental output + debounced keystroke work.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flexbox with flex-wrap for 1D control rows; set min-inline-size:0 / overflow-wrap on flex/grid/table items holding long unbreakable content; use repeat(auto-fit, minmax(...)) for responsive control/candidate/column grids.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the mode toggle, sample chips and readout are flex-wrap rows; the controls grid, candidate list and wild/multi-model columns use auto-fit minmax; the at-a-glance table cells and loader detail get overflow-wrap:anywhere so the long HF model id never forces horizontal overflow on mobile (the concrete bug fixed: 87px → 3px).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: agent overflow measurement at 360×740 DPR3 (87→3px model page, 0 on ladder pages); responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; scratchpad/headline-shots/

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Expose accessible names on all controls; use aria-pressed for toggle/segmented buttons; announce async state in a single polite role=status aria-live region; associate <label> with controls; use role=listbox/option for a selectable list.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — Best/Candidates is an aria-pressed segmented control; run/loader status is announced via role=status aria-live=polite; the practical candidate list is a role=listbox of keyboard-operable role=option rows with aria-selected; the timeline canvas has an aria-label; every slider/textarea has a native <label>.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance buttons-have-names=pass, live-status-region=pass; keyboard Enter/Space candidate selection verified in code + ladder pages load with 0 console errors

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy JS off the main thread and keep interaction handlers short to protect INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — model download + beam/sampling generation run in a Web Worker; the best headline streams incrementally; per-keystroke 'fed string' work is debounced and O(input).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; worker.js drives pipeline() with a TextStreamer; headless run showed a responsive UI during generation

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break up / bound long main-thread loops so they don't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — generation is off-thread and streamed; the candidate loop runs in the worker; there is no long synchronous main-thread loop; token-chip render is bounded by the short input length.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: headline.js renderTokens over input tokens only; streaming callbacks update the DOM in small increments

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue-to-page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inherited from public/styles.css @view-transition navigation:auto (invariant 6); use-case cards use the shared .model-card grid.

openQuestions: Is t5-small at q8 the best runnable title model, or would the apache-2.0 tarekziade/t5-small-headline-generator-sft ONNX give better headlines at similar size (a possible additive comparison)?
Should the practical page persist a chosen headline / support regenerating a single candidate in place, or does the current batch-then-pick flow best teach the sampling idea?
Would a NEW additive demo that headlines a live-pasted RSS/article feed be worthwhile, or does the multi-model summarise→headline page already cover the 'long real content' case?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass for headline-generation is done (desktop real-output + cached-auto-init run, mobile/desktop light+dark screenshots read, overflow fixed); support flipped to ok. Re-audit if the design system changes.

Consider a NEW additive demo comparing two runnable title-gen ONNX models (ldenoue vs tarekziade) on the same article, to show how fine-tune/data choice changes headline style (new slug, never replacing this page).
Re-check monoT5: if a monoT5 / mt5-mmarco checkpoint ever ships a transformers.js-compatible ONNX export, build the blocked monot5-reranker generative-reranking demo (P(true) vs cross-encoder logit).

summary: Revision-1 build critique for headline-generation (text2text-generation, WASM q8, ldenoue/Title_Generation_T5Small_Model), evidenced by a real headless full-lifecycle run: first-visit download → REAL headline 'Web platform keeps gaining new capabilities'; a warm-cache reload auto-inits with no Download button and 0 console errors; sampling produces 4 distinct candidate headlines. The demo teaches title generation as a generation problem — one beam 'best' line vs a spread of sampled candidates (the A/B use case), with the fed string + SentencePiece tokens + decode timeline as the see-inside surface. Ladder: basics (article→title), practical (A/B candidate picker), wild (clickbait-vs-neutral temperature contrast), multi-model (DistilBART summarise → T5 headline). Conformance auto-checkable pass + manual-evidenced; a mobile overflow bug from the long model id was fixed in place (87→3px); responsive desktop+mobile clean, light+dark legible. Distinct from the summarisation, FLAN-T5, and T5-base demos. (Companion task: monot5-reranker recorded blocked — no monoT5 ONNX exists.)
